reforms.description: The KiwiSaver scheme was introduced to encourage New Zealanders to save for retirement through investment-based personal retirement accounts, featuring automatic enrolment and government contributions. This innovative approach draws on insights from behavioral economics and aims to enhance private savings among New Zealanders.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging savings can lead to increased investment in the economy, supporting overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been actively in place since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of the KiwiSaver scheme represents a significant structural change in retirement savings policy.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced the KiwiSaver scheme. Starting in July 2007, it creates investment-based personal retirement accounts on a defined-contribution basis.

reforms.description: The government proposed targeted tax credits for firms investing in R&D, export market development activities, and skills training, marking a shift from a broad tax base to support innovation. This initiative aims to encourage businesses to engage in activities that generate wider benefits for the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D investment, the policy is expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of targeted tax credits represents a significant reform in the approach to supporting innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has recently proposed targeted tax credits for firms that invest in R&D, exporters, or those that provide skills training.

reforms.description: The Market Development Assistance Scheme was boosted with NZD33.75 million to support firms in international market expenditure, covering up to 50% of eligible costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting firms in international markets can enhance their competitiveness and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding boost has been actively implemented to support firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in funding for the scheme represents a significant enhancement of existing support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: In November 2006, a NZD33.75 million boost was announced to the Market Development Assistance Scheme.

reforms.description: The Global Technology Partnership scheme has been launched to improve New Zealand firms’ ability to access researchers worldwide to solve specific problems, fostering collaboration between universities and private firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to global research expertise can enhance innovation capabilities within local firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been actively launched and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The launch of the scheme represents a significant step in enhancing collaboration between sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The Global Technology Partnership scheme has been launched to improve New Zealand firms’ ability to access researchers worldwide to solve specific problems.

reforms.description: The Capitalising on R&D Action Group (CRAG) has been launched to develop and oversee follow-up actions aimed at enhancing the effectiveness of R&D investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the effectiveness of R&D investments can lead to better innovation outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The group has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of CRAG represents a significant initiative to improve R&D investment strategies.
reforms.source_quote: The Capitalising on R&D Action Group (CRAG) has been launched to develop and oversee follow-up actions.

reforms.description: The New Zealand government established the NZ Venture Investment Fund (NZVIF) in 2002 with NZD100 million of committed capital to encourage the development of a local venture-capital industry, investing in privately managed venture capital funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging the development of a local venture-capital industry can enhance funding availability for startups and innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been actively operational since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the NZVIF represents a significant structural change in the support for venture capital in New Zealand.
reforms.source_quote: The government established the NZ Venture Investment Fund (NZVIF) in 2002 with NZD100 million of committed capital.

reforms.description: The Seed Co-investment Fund was established in 2005 to provide finance for seed-stage and start-up investments, with NZD40 million available for investment over around a six-year period.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing finance for seed-stage investments can help foster the growth of new startups and innovative firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund has been actively operational since its establishment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant enhancement of support for early-stage investments.
reforms.source_quote: The NZVIF is also responsible for implementing the Seed Co-investment Fund, established in 2005, to provide finance for seed-stage and start-up investments.
